analysis.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully concluded with four astronauts splashing down in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego, California on Friday evening, April 10, 2026, after a 10-day journey around the moon. The crew — Commander Reid Wiseman, pilot Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Canadian Jeremy Hansen — traveled farther from Earth than any humans in history. Astronauts were subsequently airlifted by helicopter to the USS John P. Murtha for medical evaluation.
